trans-unit.source: How to plan a GIS Training
trans-unit.target: How to plan a GIS Training

trans-unit.source: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__
trans-unit.target: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__

trans-unit.source: If you are running a QGIS training course for the first time, there are several practical considerations that you need to keep in mind. this article, provides practical experience that complements the training material that can found in the platform, in addition to advice on how to plan a training session.
trans-unit.target: If you are running a QGIS training course for the first time, there are several practical considerations that you need to keep in mind. this article, provides practical experience that complements the training material that can found in the platform, in addition to advice on how to plan a training session.

trans-unit.source: __Before you start preparing the training schedule, there are three things to consider:__
trans-unit.target: __Before you start preparing the training schedule, there are three things to consider:__

trans-unit.source: To facilitate the planning, you can use the following list as a guide:
trans-unit.target: To facilitate the planning, you can use the following list as a guide:

trans-unit.source: 1. Preparation
trans-unit.target: 1. Preparation

trans-unit.source: Before you plan your training, you need to make some basic decisions about the setting:
trans-unit.target: Before you plan your training, you need to make some basic decisions about the setting:

trans-unit.source: Check your audience: What is the skill level and the background of your participants? Are they flexible about training times? How long can they stay away from their job? You should plan your training according to the (practical) needs of your participants to ensure that all interested participants can attend.
trans-unit.target: Check your audience: What is the skill level and the background of your participants? Are they flexible about training times? How long can they stay away from their job? You should plan your training according to the (practical) needs of your participants to ensure that all interested participants can attend.

trans-unit.source: Think about the size of the group: experience shows that 10 to 15 participants are ideal. If more people are interested in the training, consider organizing more than one training. If possible, have two trainers, so that one can present the content and the other handle administrative issues and support the participants. This is critical when the group might have varying skill and experience levels.
trans-unit.target: Think about the size of the group: experience shows that 10 to 15 participants are ideal. If more people are interested in the training, consider organizing more than one training. If possible, have two trainers, so that one can present the content and the other handle administrative issues and support the participants. This is critical when the group might have varying skill and experience levels.

trans-unit.source: The setting: Are you planning an online or face-to-face course? Will you plan the training in half-day or full-day sessions? Will the training take place on consecutive days or spread over several weeks? These questions should be answered depending on the background and needs of the participants.
trans-unit.target: The setting: Are you planning an online or face-to-face course? Will you plan the training in half-day or full-day sessions? Will the training take place on consecutive days or spread over several weeks? These questions should be answered depending on the background and needs of the participants.

trans-unit.source: Trainer handover: if multiple trainers are facilitating a GIS training, consider planning some time between sessions so that a proper debrief can be done. This ensures that all facilitators are equipped with the proper information for their session and the training can have a good continuous flow.
trans-unit.target: Trainer handover: if multiple trainers are facilitating a GIS training, consider planning some time between sessions so that a proper debrief can be done. This ensures that all facilitators are equipped with the proper information for their session and the training can have a good continuous flow.

trans-unit.source: 2. Planning
trans-unit.target: 2. Planning

trans-unit.source: You can use the platform's modular structure and the [skill levels](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/intro.html#learning-objectives) as a guide when planning your training schedule. On a practical level, you should consider the following:
trans-unit.target: You can use the platform's modular structure and the [skill levels](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/intro.html#learning-objectives) as a guide when planning your training schedule. On a practical level, you should consider the following:

trans-unit.source: Define the learning objectives in detail and communicate them at the beginning of the training. Organizing the material is easier if you know what you want to achieve. The participants need to know what they are expected to learn and where they are during the training process.
trans-unit.target: Define the learning objectives in detail and communicate them at the beginning of the training. Organizing the material is easier if you know what you want to achieve. The participants need to know what they are expected to learn and where they are during the training process.

trans-unit.source: When planning the structure of your training, you can use the sample structure as a guide, but you can also adapt it to the specific needs of your participants. If possible, use material and exercises that are related to the participant’s background.
trans-unit.target: When planning the structure of your training, you can use the sample structure as a guide, but you can also adapt it to the specific needs of your participants. If possible, use material and exercises that are related to the participant’s background.

trans-unit.source: Allow time for questions and answers. Participants usually benefit most from a trainer’s support to acute problems.
trans-unit.target: Allow time for questions and answers. Participants usually benefit most from a trainer’s support to acute problems.

trans-unit.source: Consider the practical setting: If you are organizing a face-to-face training, do you have a suitable room? What is available to them?
trans-unit.target: Consider the practical setting: If you are organizing a face-to-face training, do you have a suitable room? What is available to them?

trans-unit.source: Finally, how do you promote your training? Can you promote it within your institution? How will you organize the enrolment process? What information do you need from your participants (background, interests, previous skills)? How do you select participants?
trans-unit.target: Finally, how do you promote your training? Can you promote it within your institution? How will you organize the enrolment process? What information do you need from your participants (background, interests, previous skills)? How do you select participants?

trans-unit.source: 3. Follow-up
trans-unit.target: 3. Follow-up

trans-unit.source: During and after the training, you can support the process by encouraging and facilitating further exchanges between participants and by evaluating the training.
trans-unit.target: During and after the training, you can support the process by encouraging and facilitating further exchanges between participants and by evaluating the training.

trans-unit.source: Encourage participants to communicate and stay in touch, to exchange ideas and experiences after the training. That way, participants can support and motivate each other by sharing their experiences and successes.
trans-unit.target: Encourage participants to communicate and stay in touch, to exchange ideas and experiences after the training. That way, participants can support and motivate each other by sharing their experiences and successes.

trans-unit.source: Plan to evaluate your training. This can help you improve your training and include the participant’s perspectives.
trans-unit.target: Plan to evaluate your training. This can help you improve your training and include the participant’s perspectives.

trans-unit.source: To assess the success of the training and the learning progress of the participants, conduct an assessment before and after a training. You can use the [surveys on this platform](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_assessment.md) as a guide..
trans-unit.target: To assess the success of the training and the learning progress of the participants, conduct an assessment before and after a training. You can use the [surveys on this platform](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_assessment.md) as a guide..
